# UC-22.1.20 · GDPR Legitimate Interest Balancing Test Evidence

## Description

Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)) is the most commonly misused legal basis — regulators have fined LinkedIn €310M and others hundreds of millions for relying on legitimate interest without proper balancing tests. This use case tracks which processing activities use legitimate interest as their legal basis, monitors whether balancing test documentation exists and is current, and detects processing scope creep beyond the original legitimate interest assessment — addressing the enforcement area that generates the highest fines in 2025-2026.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for GDPR compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Tag ROPA entries using legitimate interest as legal basis; (2) maintain `gdpr_lia_register.csv` linking processing activities to Legitimate Interest Assessments; (3) alert on processing activities relying on legitimate interest without a completed LIA; (4) track data subject objections (Art. 21) per processing activity — high objection rates signal the balancing test may be failing; (5) flag LIAs not reviewed in >12 months; (6) stop processing immediately for any activity where LIA concludes "not justified."

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `gdpr_ropa_register.csv`, `gdpr_lia_register.csv` (Legitimate Interest Assessments), CIM Network_Traffic data model.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Tag ROPA entries using legitimate interest as legal basis; (2) maintain `gdpr_lia_register.csv` linking processing activities to Legitimate Interest Assessments; (3) alert on processing activities relying on legitimate interest without a completed LIA; (4) track data subject objections (Art. 21) per processing activity — high objection rates signal the balancing test may be failing; (5) flag LIAs not reviewed in >12 months; (6) stop processing immediately for any activity where LIA concludes…

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup gdpr_ropa_register.csv WHERE legal_basis="legitimate_interest"
| lookup gdpr_lia_register.csv processing_activity OUTPUT lia_status, lia_date, lia_reviewer, lia_outcome, data_subject_objections
| eval lia_coverage=case(
    lia_status="completed" AND lia_outcome="justified", "JUSTIFIED",
    lia_status="completed" AND lia_outcome="not_justified", "STOP_PROCESSING",
    lia_status="in_progress", "IN_PROGRESS",
    isnull(lia_status), "MISSING — LIA REQUIRED")
| eval review_overdue=if(isnotnull(lia_date) AND (now()-strptime(lia_date,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400 > 365, "REVIEW_DUE", "OK")
| eval objection_rate=if(isnotnull(data_subject_objections) AND data_subject_objections > 10, "HIGH_OBJECTIONS — reassess", "Normal")
| where lia_coverage IN ("MISSING — LIA REQUIRED", "STOP_PROCESSING") OR review_overdue="REVIEW_DUE" OR objection_rate="HIGH_OBJECTIONS — reassess"
| table processing_activity, system_name, lia_coverage, lia_date, review_overdue, data_subject_objections, objection_rate
| sort lia_coverage
```

Understanding this SPL

**GDPR Legitimate Interest Balancing Test Evidence (Art. 6(1)(f))** — Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)) is the most commonly misused legal basis — regulators have fined LinkedIn €310M and others hundreds of millions for relying on legitimate interest without proper balancing tests. This use case tracks which processing activities use legitimate interest as their legal basis, monitors whether balancing test documentation exists and is current, and detects processing scope creep beyond the original legitimate interest assessment — addressing…

Documented **Data sources**: `gdpr_ropa_register.csv`, `gdpr_lia_register.csv` (Legitimate Interest Assessments), CIM Network_Traffic data model. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **lia_coverage** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **review_overdue** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **objection_rate** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where lia_coverage IN ("MISSING — LIA REQUIRED", "STOP_PROCESSING") OR review_overdue="REVIEW_DUE" OR objection_rate="HIGH_…` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: data_subject_objections, lia_coverage, lia_date, objection_rate, processing_activity, review_overdue, system_name.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (LIA coverage gaps), Pie chart (LIA status distribution), Bar chart (objections by processing activity), Single value (missing LIAs count).

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup gdpr_ropa_register.csv WHERE legal_basis="legitimate_interest"
| lookup gdpr_lia_register.csv processing_activity OUTPUT lia_status, lia_date, lia_reviewer, lia_outcome, data_subject_objections
| eval lia_coverage=case(
    lia_status="completed" AND lia_outcome="justified", "JUSTIFIED",
    lia_status="completed" AND lia_outcome="not_justified", "STOP_PROCESSING",
    lia_status="in_progress", "IN_PROGRESS",
    isnull(lia_status), "MISSING — LIA REQUIRED")
| eval review_overdue=if(isnotnull(lia_date) AND (now()-strptime(lia_date,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400 > 365, "REVIEW_DUE", "OK")
| eval objection_rate=if(isnotnull(data_subject_objections) AND data_subject_objections > 10, "HIGH_OBJECTIONS — reassess", "Normal")
| where lia_coverage IN ("MISSING — LIA REQUIRED", "STOP_PROCESSING") OR review_overdue="REVIEW_DUE" OR objection_rate="HIGH_OBJECTIONS — reassess"
| table processing_activity, system_name, lia_coverage, lia_date, review_overdue, data_subject_objections, objection_rate
| sort lia_coverage
```

## Visualization

Table (LIA coverage gaps), Pie chart (LIA status distribution), Bar chart (objections by processing activity), Single value (missing LIAs count). Key fields: `data_subject_objections`, `lia_coverage`, `lia_date`, `objection_rate`, `processing_activity`, `review_overdue`.
